name: jacob-skills
description: Jacob's research and manuscript-revision workflow skills.

plugins.name: research-27
plugins.description: Citation-rich literature-review brief generator with verify-and-loop verification.

plugins.name: citation-deepening
plugins.description: Pulls verbatim source quotes for fact-checking citations in a manuscript.

plugins.name: source-quality-check
plugins.description: Rates manuscript references on venue tier, peer-review status, recency, and author credibility.

plugins.name: commented-edit-roundtrip
plugins.description: Round-trip editing between Claude and the user using Word margin comments (perpetual-inbox + one-shot-rewrite modes).

plugins.name: revision-queue
plugins.description: Two-file open-discussion + chronological-changelog system for multi-round revisions. Pairs with commented-edit-roundtrip.

plugins.name: tony-github-push
plugins.description: Push manuscript edits to a configured remote/branch (paths set via env or CLAUDE.md, not hardcoded).

plugins.name: calendar-search
plugins.description: Multi-calendar Google Calendar lookup with bilingual EN/ZH keyword expansion, chunked windows for noisy calendars, and source-timezone preservation. Cowork-only (Jacob does not install on Claude Code). Configured for Jacob's calendar set — fork and adapt for yours.

plugins.name: sync-cowork-skill
plugins.description: One-way publish of a Cowork-side skill to its marketplace plugin folder, with mandatory diff + sensitive-content scan + explicit confirm gate. Run from Claude Code only. Jacob-specific (assumes ~/jacob-skills/ as the marketplace clone).

plugins.name: claude-env-sync
plugins.description: Publish your Claude Code environment as a redacted snapshot to your marketplace repo, and on the import side, interactively diff a published snapshot against your own environment and pick what to take. Replaces the older jacob-environment approach (which stacked hooks instead of comparing). Generic — works for any pair of collaborators.

plugins.name: project-filename
plugins.description: Per-project output filename convention `<role> [<project>].<ext>`. Called by consumer skills (revision-queue, commented-edit-roundtrip, citation-deepening) to resolve filenames. Lookup-first: existing filenames in the project root are the source of truth, so shorthand stays consistent across sessions without env vars.

plugins.name: project-map
plugins.description: Creates and maintains MAP.md files — project-navigation files at folder roots that orient Claude (and humans) to what's where, who authored it, and what's source/draft/canonical. Trigger on 'set up a MAP', 'create a project map', 'make a MAP.md', new project orientation when none exists, or stale MAPs after file moves/renames. Standardizes on MAP.md (retires the legacy INDEX.md name, no (claude) suffix). Location encodes scope: one at the project root, optional in complex subfolders.

plugins.name: chat-substitute-html
plugins.description: Conventions for HTMLs that stand in for chat — pin, what's-new surface, locked-decision economy, bi-file iteration pattern. Used when Claude's reply to Jacob would be too long for chat. Personalized to Jacob (UChicago social-psych researcher); fork and adapt for yours.

plugins.name: decision-forms-html
plugins.description: Interactive decision-form pattern for HTML pages — radios, comment textareas, sticky Copy/Download/Clear toolbar, localStorage persistence. Composable into chat-substitute HTMLs, literature reviews, mockups, anywhere Claude needs answers back.

plugins.name: jacob-todos
plugins.description: Manage Jacob's personal to-do system, built on Google Calendar + an HTML check-in surface with tasks_v3.json as the single source of truth. Triggers on consolidation, ranking, adding or merging tasks, the morning check-in, suggested-plan generation, and the scheduled todo-check-in routine.

plugins.name: paper-search
plugins.description: ALWAYS invoke when the user asks to search/browse/find academic literature on a topic. Searches arXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, CrossRef across 20+ sources. Skip ONLY for: fetching a specific named paper (use paper-retrieval), verifying citation metadata (use citation-checker), or full research briefs (use research-27).

plugins.name: paper-retrieval
plugins.description: Use when the user asks to find or download the PDF of a specific named academic paper by title, author, or DOI. Triggers on "download paper X", "find/get the PDF of Y", "fetch this paper", or upgrading an ABSTRACT-ONLY reference to FULL-PDF.

plugins.name: markdown-report-builder
plugins.description: Build polished `.docx` (via pandoc reference doc) and `.pdf` (via Eisvogel LaTeX template) from a markdown source. Cross-project; one-time setup per project, then `./reports/build.sh report.md` produces a clean Word + PDF pair.

plugins.name: vector-stimulus-design
plugins.description: Use when generating parameterized research stimuli from designer vector source files (Adobe Illustrator .ai, .svg). Covers lossless extraction, shape editing, color permutation, rasterization, reproducibility, and the review workflow for collaborating with a researcher on stimulus choice.

plugins.name: migrate-claude-chats
plugins.description: Transfer/migrate Claude Code chat history from one Mac (or user account) to another and make the moved chats work in agent view. Fixes the cwd-rewrite trap that causes "Session can't start" / "session is not responding" crash-loops on /bg. Ships scripts/fix_chat_cwd.py (dry-run, per-file safety gate, stale bridge-marker cleanup).

plugins.name: wrap-root-session
plugins.description: Use when a Claude Code session run from ~/Claude (the workspace root) is ending and its artifacts sit loose — new files at root, in Unordered/, or scattered across folders — and Jacob wants the session's work tidied into one self-contained folder. Triggers: "organize this session's files into a folder", "wrap up this session", "I'm going to close this session", "/wrap-root-session". Also fires proactively at session close when this session created root-level or Unordered/ files on a topic with no owning folder.
